title: For a Hard Decision
scripture_text: If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask God, who gives generously to all without reproach.
reflection: Most decisions are not between right and wrong. They are between two goods, or two harms. Be gentle with yourself.
body:
You have done the work. You have weighed the options. You have asked the people you trust. The answer is still not clear.

That is okay. Most real decisions are made in the fog, not the clarity. Faith is not the absence of fog. Faith is one more step in it.

Ask the Lord. Not for a sign. For wisdom. He gives it generously, James says, without scolding. He will give it to you. Probably not as a flash of lightning. Probably as a slow settling, a quiet rightness, a few weeks later when you look back and see.

Decide. Live into the decision. The Lord will meet you there.
prayer: Lord, I cannot see the path. Give me wisdom. Give me courage. Give me peace with whichever door I walk through. Amen.
